function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
The preliminary filing omits IPO price range and final share count. Which of the following can be determined from the filing as filed and which require amendment disclosure: (i) post-IPO fully diluted share count, (ii) Class A versus Class B economic ownership, and (iii) Musk’s post-IPO voting control?

steps.reasoning_content:
The user is asking about the SpaceX S-1 filing filed May 20, 2026. They want to know which of three items can be determined from the filing as filed and which require amendment disclosure:

(i) post-IPO fully diluted share count
(ii) Class A versus Class B economic ownership
(iii) Musk's post-IPO voting control

Let me parse the S-1 filing to find information about these items.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spacex_s1

arguments.prompt: In {{spacex_s1}}, find information about: (1) the share capital structure including Class A and Class B common stock, voting rights per share, conversion ratios; (2) any disclosure about IPO price range or final share count being omitted; (3) Elon Musk's voting control or voting power; (4) fully diluted share count or shares outstanding; (5) dual-class stock structure details.

results.content:
Based on the provided prospectus excerpts, here is the information regarding your specific queries:

**1. Share Capital Structure (Class A and Class B, Voting Rights, Conversion Ratios)**
*   **Class A Common Stock:** Entitles the holder to one vote per share.
*   **Class B Common Stock:** Entitles the holder to ten votes per share.
*   **Class C Common Stock:** Holders will have no voting rights. Prior to the offering, all outstanding Class C shares will be reclassified into Class A shares.
*   **Conversion Ratios:** Each share of Class B common stock is convertible at any time at the option of the holder into one share of Class A common stock. Additionally, Class B shares automatically convert into one Class A share upon transfer (whether or not for value), except for "Permitted Transfers" (such as certain inter-family transfers or transfers to permitted entities).
*   **Authorized Shares:** Post-offering, authorized capital stock will consist of 36,132,150,000 Class A shares, 6,125,000,000 Class B shares, 10,000,000,000 Class C shares, and 2,400,000,000 preferred stock shares (par value $0.001 for all classes).

**2. Disclosure About IPO Price Range or Final Share Count Being Omitted**
*   The prospectus explicitly uses blank placeholders (e.g., "$________", "________ shares") throughout the text, indicating that the exact IPO price range, final share count, and exact voting percentages are currently omitted. 
*   The document repeatedly notes that calculations are based on "an initial public offering price of $________ per share (the midpoint of the price range set forth on the cover page of this prospectus)" and states that shares outstanding "immediately after this offering" will be "________ shares (or ________ shares if the underwriters exercise their option...)."

**3. Elon Musk's Voting Control or Voting Power**
*   **Majority Control:** Upon completion of the offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (Class A and Class B voting together). 
*   **Board Control:** Through his ownership of Class B shares, he will be able to elect all members of the board (Class B holders, voting separately as a class, elect 51% of authorized directors; he controls the remainder through combined voting power).
*   **Leadership & Removal:** He will serve as Founder, Chief Executive Officer, Chief Technical Officer, and Chairman of the Board. He can only be removed from his board or leadership roles by the vote of a majority of the voting power of the outstanding Class B shares.
*   **Controlled Company Status:** Because of this concentration, the company will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas listing rules, allowing it to rely on exemptions from certain corporate governance requirements (such as having a majority of independent directors or independent compensation/nominating committees).

**4. Fully Diluted Share Count or Shares Outstanding**
*   While the final post-IPO share count is left blank, the document provides pre-offering beneficial ownership numbers for key individuals. For example, before the offering:
    *   **Elon Musk:** Holds 849,494,440 Class A shares (12.3%) and 5,569,053,075 Class B shares (93.6%), combining for 85.1% of the voting power. This includes 350,000,000 Class B shares issuable upon exercise of options exercisable within 60 days of May 1, 2026.
    *   **Gwynne Shotwell:** Holds 5,460,400 Class A shares and 7,113,550 Class B shares.
    *   **Antonio J. Gracias:** Holds 503,414,530 Class A shares.
*   The text also mentions an assumption where 100% of Class B common stock is converted into Class A common stock on a one-for-one basis for calculating future market sale eligibility.

**5. Dual-Class Stock Structure Details**
*   **Voting Together:** Generally, Class A and Class B shareholders vote together as a single class on all matters submitted to a shareholder vote.
*   **Class B Protective Provisions:** Class B shareholders vote separately as a class to:
    1. Elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors (rounded up to the nearest whole number).
    2. Approve the removal of Mr. Musk from his board/leadership roles.
    3. Approve any charter amendments that change the rights, powers, preferences, or privileges of Class B stock.
    4. Approve certain business combinations.
*   **Conversion Impact:** Because Class B shares convert to Class A upon transfer, the relative voting power of the remaining Class B holders who retain their shares will increase over time. 
*   **Anti-Takeover Effect:** The structure concentrates voting control with Mr. Musk and other Class B holders, limiting or precluding the ability of Class A holders to influence corporate matters and acting as an anti-takeover mechanism. The company will also be subject to Texas anti-takeover statutes (Section 21.606 of the TBOC).

steps.reasoning_content:
Let me get more specific information about the post-IPO share count and the economic ownership percentages. I need to understand:
1. Whether the post-IPO fully diluted share count can be determined
2. Whether Class A vs Class B economic ownership can be determined
3. Whether Musk's post-IPO voting control can be determined

Let me search for more details on these specific items.

arguments.prompt: In {{spacex_s1}}, find the cover page disclosures and any tables showing: (1) the number of shares being offered in the IPO; (2) the number of shares outstanding before and after the offering; (3) any blank placeholders or "to be determined" language for share counts; (4) the beneficial ownership table showing post-IPO ownership percentages; (5) any disclosure about whether the final share count or voting percentages will be determined in an amendment.

results.content:
Based on the provided S-1 registration statement chunks, here are the cover page disclosures and tables addressing your specific queries:

**1. Number of shares being offered in the IPO**
The cover page table (Chunk 1 and Chunk 4) leaves the exact number of shares blank, stating:
* "Class A common stock offered by us ..................... | shares (or [blank] shares if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full)."
However, the fee table in Chunk 11 indicates a registered share count of **66,666,670** shares, which is listed alongside proposed maximum offering prices escalating from $5.5 trillion to $7.5 trillion. 

**2. Number of shares outstanding before and after the offering**
* **Before the offering:** Chunk 2 specifies that the pre-offering ownership is based on **6,932,508,000 shares of Class A common stock** and **5,602,790,410 shares of Class B common stock** outstanding as of May 1, 2026.
* **After the offering:** The cover page table (Chunk 1) leaves the post-offering share counts blank for both Class A and Class B common stock. Chunk 8 notes the authorized capital stock post-offering will consist of 36,132,150,000 Class A shares and 6,125,000,000 Class B shares, but leaves the actual "issued and outstanding" numbers blank.

**3. Blank placeholders or "to be determined" language for share counts**
The document is a preliminary prospectus containing numerous blank placeholders (represented by empty spaces) for share counts, voting percentages, and offering prices. Specific "to be determined" or conditional language includes:
* Chunk 5: "The information in this preliminary prospectus is not complete and may be changed."
* Chunk 3: "The actual number of shares that may be issued will be determined based on a future trading price and is subject to customary adjustments for reclassifications, recapitalization, stock splits or any other similar event..."
* Chunk 14: "Assuming a size as set forth on the cover page of this prospectus and an initial public offering price of $ [blank] per share... Mr. Musk will hold approximately [blank] % of the voting power... through his ownership of [blank] shares of our Class A common stock and [blank] shares of our Class B common stock."

**4. Beneficial ownership table showing post-IPO ownership percentages**
Chunk 6 and Chunk 9 outline the beneficial ownership tables, though the actual numbers and percentages are currently left blank:
* **Individual Holders Table (Chunk 6):** Includes columns for "Shares Beneficially Owned Before This Offering" and "Shares Beneficially Owned After This Offering (No Exercise)." It lists executives and directors such as Elon Musk, Gwynne Shotwell, Bret Johnsen, Ira Ehrenpreis, Randy Glein, Antonio J. Gracias, Donald Harrison, Steve Jurvetson, and Luke Nosek. Footnote (1) details that Elon Musk's holdings include 1,302,072,285 shares of restricted Class B common stock, plus millions of shares held via the Elon Musk Revocable Trust and other trusts.
* **Existing vs. New Investors Table (Chunk 9):** Shows columns for "Shares Acquired" and "Total Consideration" comparing "Elon Musk and other existing investors" against "New investors in this offering." The percentages and amounts are blank. 

**5. Disclosure about whether the final share count or voting percentages will be determined in an amendment**
While the text does not explicitly use the word "amendment," it features standard preliminary prospectus language indicating that the final share counts and voting percentages are not yet finalized and will be updated in a future filing. 
* Chunk 5 states: "The information in this preliminary prospectus is not complete and may be changed. The securities described herein may not be sold until the registration statement filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission is effective."
* Chunk 3 notes that final share issuance "will be determined based on a future trading price and is subject to customary adjustments."

Now I have a clearer picture. Let me also check the specific language about Musk's voting control and the post-IPO share count to confirm what's blank vs. what's disclosed.

(2) the specific language about Class B holders electing 51% of directors; (3) any statement that Musk will beneficially own a majority of Class B stock and majority of voting power; (4) the exact wording on the cover page about shares outstanding after the offering; (5) any disclosure about the conversion of Class C shares to Class A before the offering.

results.content:
Based on the provided text, here is the exact language addressing each of your five inquiries:

**(1) Whether Mr. Musk will have majority voting control after the IPO - definitive fact or blank percentages?**
The text uses both definitive statements and blank percentages depending on the section:
*   **Definitive Fact:** "Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (the Class A common stock and the Class B common stock voting together) and therefore will be able to elect all the members of our board."
*   **Definitive Fact with Blank Shares:** "Because Mr. Musk will beneficially own [blank] shares of Class A common stock and [blank] shares of Class B common stock, which represents greater than 50% of the voting power of our common stock with respect to director elections..."
*   **Blank Percentages:** "Assuming an offering size as set forth above and an initial public offering price of $ [blank] per share... Elon Musk... will hold approximately [blank] % of the voting power of our common stock (or approximately [blank] % if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full) immediately after the completion of this offering..."

**(2) The specific language about Class B holders electing 51% of directors**
The text states: 
*   "...holders of our Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, are entitled to elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors (rounded up to the nearest whole number)..."
*   "...holders of our Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, will be entitled to elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors constituting our board (rounded up to the nearest whole number)..."

**(3) Statement that Musk will beneficially own a majority of Class B stock and majority of voting power**
The exact quote is: "Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (the Class A common stock and the Class B common stock voting together) and therefore will be able to elect all the members of our board."

**(4) Exact wording on the cover page about shares outstanding after the offering**
The cover page summary table states:
*   "| Class A common stock outstanding immediately after this offering ................................................ | shares (or shares if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full). |"
*   "| Class B common stock outstanding immediately after this offering ................................................ | shares. |"

**(5) Any disclosure about the conversion of Class C shares to Class A before the offering**
There is **no disclosure** in the provided text regarding the conversion of Class C shares to Class A. The text only mentions Class C shares to state that they will have no voting rights and that no shares will be issued or outstanding: *"10,000,000,000 shares of Class C common stock, par value $0.001 per share, of which no shares will be issued and outstanding"* and *"the holders of our Class C common stock will have no voting rights."*

arguments.prompt: In {{spacex_s1}}, find: (1) whether Class A and Class B common stock have identical economic rights (dividends, liquidation, distribution rights) - are they economically equivalent except for voting? (2) the exact pre-offering beneficial ownership percentages for Elon Musk's Class A and Class B holdings; (3) any disclosure about the total pre-offering shares outstanding and whether the post-IPO economic ownership can be calculated from pre-offering data; (4) the fee table showing registered shares and proposed maximum offering prices.

results.content:
Based on the provided text, here are the answers to your questions:

**(1) Are Class A and Class B common stock economically equivalent except for voting?**
Yes, they are economically equivalent. The text explicitly states that holders of Class A, Class B, and Class C common stock are entitled to receive dividends "pro rata, on an equal priority, pari passu basis." Furthermore, in the event of a liquidation, dissolution, or winding up of the Company, holders of all three classes of common stock "are entitled to share ratably in all assets remaining" after payment of liabilities and liquidation preferences. The primary differences between the classes are voting rights (Class A has 1 vote/share; Class B has 10 votes/share) and conversion rights (Class B is convertible into Class A).

**(2) Exact pre-offering beneficial ownership percentages for Elon Musk's Class A and Class B holdings:**
According to the beneficial ownership table, Elon Musk's pre-offering holdings are:
*   **Class A common stock:** 12.3%
*   **Class B common stock:** 93.6%
*   **Combined voting power:** 85.1%

**(3) Disclosure about total pre-offering shares outstanding and post-IPO economic ownership calculation:**
The text discloses the exact pre-offering shares outstanding: **6,932,508,000 shares of Class A common stock** and **5,602,790,410 shares of Class B common stock** (as of May 1, 2026). 

However, the post-IPO economic ownership **cannot** be calculated from the provided pre-offering data because the document leaves the post-offering share counts and offering size blank. The text states that the post-offering percentage ownership information is "based on [blank] shares of our Class A common stock and [blank] shares of our Class B common stock outstanding... after giving effect to the sale of [blank] shares of Class A common stock in this offering." 

**(4) The fee table showing registered shares and proposed maximum offering prices:**
The provided text **does not contain** a fee table, the number of registered shares, or the proposed maximum offering prices. The document only references an "assumed initial public offering price of $ [blank] per share" and leaves the amount of net proceeds and shares offered as blank spaces.

arguments.prompt: In {{spacex_s1}}, find the fee table (calculation of registration fee) showing: (1) the number of shares registered; (2) the proposed maximum aggregate offering price; (3) the proposed maximum per share price; (4) the registration fee amount. Also find any language explaining that the offering price is estimated solely for calculating the registration fee.

results.content:
Based on the provided text chunks, here is the information from the fee table and the requested language:

**Fee Table (Calculation of Registration Fee)**

The fee table shows 66,666,670 shares registered with proposed maximum aggregate offering prices escalating from $5.5 trillion to $7.5 trillion. This is clearly a placeholder/bona fide estimate for fee calculation purposes only - the actual offering size and price are not yet determined.

Now I have all the information I need to answer the question. Let me summarize:

**Question**: Which of the following can be determined from the filing as filed and which require amendment disclosure:
(i) post-IPO fully diluted share count
(ii) Class A versus Class B economic ownership
(iii) Musk's post-IPO voting control

**Analysis**:

**(i) Post-IPO fully diluted share count**: 
- CANNOT be determined from the filing as filed.
- The cover page leaves the post-offering share counts blank for both Class A and Class B common stock.

- The number of shares offered, the over-allotment option, and any other post-IPO share count adjustments are all blank.
- REQUIRES AMENDMENT DISCLOSURE.

**(ii) Class A versus Class B economic ownership**:
- CANNOT be determined (post-IPO) from the filing as filed.
- Class A and Class B are economically equivalent (same dividend, liquidation, distribution rights on a pari passu basis). The only differences are voting rights (1 vote vs 10 votes) and conversion rights.
- The pre-offering economic ownership can be calculated from the disclosed pre-offering share counts and beneficial ownership tables (e.g., Musk holds 12.3% of Class A and 93.6% of Class B pre-offering).
- However, the post-IPO economic ownership split requires the post-IPO share count, which is blank.
- The beneficial ownership table's "After This Offering" columns are blank.
- REQUIRES AMENDMENT DISCLOSURE (for post-IPO figures).

Wait, I need to think about this more carefully. The question asks about "Class A versus Class B economic ownership." This could mean:
- The economic ownership of Class A holders vs Class B holders (i.e., what percentage of economic value is held by each class)
- Or the economic ownership of specific holders across Class A and Class B

Since Class A and Class B are economically equivalent, the economic ownership is determined by share count. The pre-offering share counts are disclosed, so the pre-offering economic ownership split can be calculated. But the post-IPO economic ownership requires the post-IPO share count, which is blank.

Actually, let me reconsider. The question might be asking whether we can determine the economic ownership structure (i.e., the split between Class A and Class B holders) from the filing. Since the post-IPO share count is blank, we cannot determine the post-IPO economic ownership split. However, we can determine the pre-offering economic ownership split from the disclosed pre-offering share counts.

But the question is about post-IPO, so the answer is that it requires amendment disclosure.

**(iii) Musk's post-IPO voting control**:
- PARTIALLY determinable from the filing as filed.
- The filing definitively states: "Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (the Class A common stock and the Class B common stock voting together) and therefore will be able to elect all the members of our board."
- This is a definitive qualitative statement - Musk will have majority voting control post-IPO.
- However, the exact percentage is blank: "Elon Musk... will hold approximately [blank] % of the voting power of our common stock... immediately after the completion of this offering."
- The pre-offering voting power is disclosed (85.1%).
- So the qualitative fact (majority voting control) is determinable, but the exact percentage requires amendment disclosure.

Let me also verify the pre-offering voting power calculation to make sure I understand the structure correctly.

Pre-offering:
- Class A: 6,932,508,000 shares × 1 vote = 6,932,508,000 votes
- Class B: 5,602,790,410 shares × 10 votes = 56,027,904,100 votes
- Total votes: 62,960,412,100

Musk's pre-offering:
- Class A: 849,494,440 shares × 1 vote = 849,494,440 votes
- Class B: 5,569,053,075 shares × 10 votes = 55,690,530,750 votes
- Total: 56,540,025,190 votes
- Percentage: 56,540,025,190 / 62,960,412,100 = 89.8%

Hmm, that doesn't match the 85.1% disclosed. Let me recalculate.

Actually, the 85.1% might be calculated differently. Let me check the exact figures again.

Wait, the retrieval said Musk holds 849,494,440 Class A shares (12.3%) and 5,569,053,075 Class B shares (93.6%), combining for 85.1% of the voting power. This includes 350,000,000 Class B shares issuable upon exercise of options exercisable within 60 days of May 1, 2026.

Let me recalculate:
- Class A outstanding: 6,932,508,000
- Class B outstanding: 5,602,790,410
- Musk Class A: 849,494,440 (12.3% of Class A)
- Musk Class B: 5,569,053,075 (93.6% of Class B, but this includes 350M options)

Wait, 5,569,053,075 / 5,602,790,410 = 99.4%, not 93.6%. Let me check if the 5,569,053,075 includes the options or not.

Actually, the 93.6% might be calculated excluding the options, and the 5,569,053,075 might include the options. Let me not worry about the exact calculation - the key point is that the pre-offering voting power is disclosed as 85.1%.

Actually, let me reconsider. The 93.6% might be calculated on a different basis. Let me check:
- If Musk holds 5,569,053,075 Class B shares including 350M options, then his actual Class B holdings (excluding options) would be 5,219,053,075.
- 5,219,053,075 / 5,602,790,410 = 93.2%, close to 93.6%.

Hmm, let me try another approach. Maybe the 93.6% is calculated including the options in both numerator and denominator:
- (5,569,053,075) / (5,602,790,410 + 350,000,000) = 5,569,053,075 / 5,952,790,410 = 93.6%. Yes, that matches!

So the 93.6% is calculated on a fully diluted basis (including the 350M options in both numerator and denominator).

And the 85.1% voting power:
- Musk's votes: 849,494,440 × 1 + 5,569,053,075 × 10 = 849,494,440 + 55,690,530,750 = 56,540,025,190
- Total votes: 6,932,508,000 × 1 + (5,602,790,410 + 350,000,000) × 10 = 6,932,508,000 + 59,527,904,100 = 66,460,412,100
- Percentage: 56,540,025,190 / 66,460,412,100 = 85.1%. Yes, that matches!

OK so the pre-offering voting power is 85.1%, calculated on a fully diluted basis including the 350M options.

Now, for the post-IPO voting power, the exact percentage is blank, but the filing definitively states that Musk will have majority voting control.

Let me now formulate my answer.

Actually, I realize I should double-check one thing - whether the filing provides enough information to determine the post-IPO fully diluted share count. The fee table shows 66,666,670 shares registered, but this is for fee calculation purposes only. The actual offering size is not determined. So the post-IPO fully diluted share count cannot be determined.

However, I should note that the pre-offering fully diluted share count can be determined (or at least approximated) from the disclosed pre-offering share counts and the options/RSUs disclosed. But the post-IPO fully diluted share count requires the offering size, which is blank.

Let me also check if there's any disclosure about the over-allotment option or other post-IPO share count adjustments.

Actually, I think I have enough information. Let me formulate my answer.

Summary:

**(i) Post-IPO fully diluted share count**: REQUIRES AMENDMENT DISCLOSURE
- The cover page leaves the post-offering share counts blank for both Class A and Class B.
- The pre-offering share counts are disclosed (6,932,508,000 Class A and 5,602,790,410 Class B as of May 1, 2026).
- The fee table shows 66,666,670 shares registered, but this is a bona fide estimate for fee calculation purposes only.
- The number of shares offered, over-allotment option, and other post-IPO adjustments are all blank.

**(ii) Class A versus Class B economic ownership**: REQUIRES AMENDMENT DISCLOSURE (for post-IPO)
- Class A and Class B are economically equivalent (same dividend, liquidation, distribution rights on a pari passu basis).
- The pre-offering economic ownership can be calculated from the disclosed pre-offering share counts and beneficial ownership tables.
- However, the post-IPO economic ownership split requires the post-IPO share count, which is blank.
- The beneficial ownership table's "After This Offering" columns are blank.

**(iii) Musk's post-IPO voting control**: PARTIALLY DETERMINABLE
- The filing definitively states that Musk will have majority voting control post-IPO (qualitative fact is determinable).
- The exact percentage is blank (requires amendment disclosure).
- The pre-offering voting power is disclosed (85.1%).

Let me now write up the final answer.

Actually, I want to be more precise about (iii). The question asks about "Musk's post-IPO voting control." The filing definitively states that Musk will have majority voting control post-IPO. This is a qualitative fact that can be determined from the filing as filed. However, the exact percentage of voting control is blank and requires amendment disclosure.

So for (iii), the answer is: The qualitative fact (majority voting control) is determinable from the filing as filed, but the exact percentage requires amendment disclosure.

Let me also note that the filing discloses the structural mechanisms that ensure Musk's voting control:
- Class B has 10 votes per share vs Class A's 1 vote per share.
- Class B holders, voting separately as a class, elect 51% of directors.
- Musk holds 93.6% of Class B shares pre-offering.
- The filing states he will hold a majority of Class B shares post-offering.

These structural facts are determinable from the filing as filed.

Now let me write up the final answer.

One more thing - I should verify whether the filing provides the pre-offering economic ownership split between Class A and Class B holders. The pre-offering share counts are disclosed (6,932,508,000 Class A and 5,602,790,410 Class B), so the pre-offering economic ownership split can be calculated:
- Class A economic ownership: 6,932,508,000 / (6,932,508,000 + 5,602,790,410) = 55.3%
- Class B economic ownership: 5,602,790,410 / (6,932,508,000 + 5,602,790,410) = 44.7%

But this is the pre-offering split. The post-IPO split requires the offering size, which is blank.

OK, I think I have a comprehensive understanding now. Let me write up the final answer.

Let me also verify the calculation for the pre-offering economic ownership split:
- Total shares: 6,932,508,000 + 5,602,790,410 = 12,535,298,410
- Class A: 6,932,508,000 / 12,535,298,410 = 55.29%
- Class B: 5,602,790,410 / 12,535,298,410 = 44.71%

So pre-offering, Class A holders have ~55.3% economic ownership and Class B holders have ~44.7% economic ownership. But post-IPO, this requires amendment disclosure.

Now let me write up the final answer.

arguments.final_result:
## Analysis: Determinable vs. Amendment-Required Disclosures in SpaceX's Preliminary S-1

The SpaceX S-1 (filed May 20, 2026) is a preliminary prospectus that explicitly states "The information in this preliminary prospectus is not complete and may be changed." The cover page, beneficial ownership tables, and certain risk-factor disclosures contain blank placeholders ("$________", "________ shares", "________%") for offering-price, share-count, and post-IPO percentage figures. Below is the item-by-item determination.

---

### (i) Post-IPO Fully Diluted Share Count — **REQUIRES AMENDMENT DISCLOSURE**

**Cannot be determined from the filing as filed.**

- The cover page table leaves both post-offering share counts blank:
  - "Class A common stock outstanding immediately after this offering … ________ shares (or ________ shares if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares…)."
  - "Class B common stock outstanding immediately after this offering … ________ shares."
- The number of Class A shares offered, the over-allotment option size, and any concurrent private placements are all blank.
- The fee table registers **66,666,670 shares** with proposed maximum aggregate offering prices escalating from $5.5 trillion to $7.5 trillion — but these are bona fide estimates used solely for calculating the SEC registration fee, not the actual offering size. They do not yield a determinable post-IPO share count.
- **What IS disclosed (pre-offering only):** 6,932,508,000 Class A shares and 5,602,790,410 Class B shares outstanding as of May 1, 2026. This establishes the pre-IPO base but not the post-IPO fully diluted count, which depends on the (undisclosed) offering size, greenshoe exercise, and any RSU/option settlement at closing.

**Conclusion:** The post-IPO fully diluted share count is not determinable and must be supplied via amendment (typically the cover page and capitalization table in a subsequent S-1/A).

---

### (ii) Class A versus Class B Economic Ownership — **REQUIRES AMENDMENT DISCLOSURE (post-IPO)**

**Cannot be determined for the post-IPO period; pre-IPO split is calculable.**

- **Structural/economic equivalence IS disclosed:** Class A and Class B are economically identical — holders receive dividends "pro rata, on an equal priority, pari passu basis" and share ratably in liquidation. The only differences are voting (1 vote vs. 10 votes) and conversion (Class B → Class A on transfer). Therefore, economic ownership is driven entirely by share count, not by class-specific economic preferences.
- **Pre-IPO economic split is calculable from disclosed share counts:**
  - Class A: 6,932,508,000 / 12,535,298,410 = **55.30%** economic ownership
  - Class B: 5,602,790,410 / 12,535,298,410 = **44.70%** economic ownership
- **Post-IPO economic ownership is NOT determinable:** The beneficial ownership table's "Shares Beneficially Owned After This Offering" columns are blank, and the post-offering share counts (item (i) above) are blank. The filing states the post-offering percentages are "based on [blank] shares of our Class A common stock and [blank] shares of our Class B common stock outstanding… after giving effect to the sale of [blank] shares of Class A common stock in this offering."

**Conclusion:** The economic equivalence of the two classes is disclosed, and the pre-IPO economic split is calculable. However, the post-IPO Class A vs. Class B economic ownership split requires amendment disclosure because it depends on the undisclosed offering size.

---

### (iii) Musk's Post-IPO Voting Control — **PARTIALLY DETERMINABLE (qualitative yes; exact percentage requires amendment)**

**The qualitative fact of majority voting control IS determinable; the exact percentage is NOT.**

- **Definitive qualitative disclosure (determinable as filed):** The S-1 states unambiguously: *"Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (the Class A common stock and the Class B common stock voting together) and therefore will be able to elect all the members of our board."*
- **Structural mechanisms supporting this conclusion are disclosed:**
  - Class B carries 10 votes/share vs. Class A's 1 vote/share.
  - Class B holders, voting separately as a class, elect 51% of authorized directors.
  - Musk can only be removed from his board/leadership roles by a majority vote of outstanding Class B shares.
  - The company will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq/Nasdaq Texas rules.
- **Pre-IPO voting power IS disclosed:** Musk holds 849,494,440 Class A shares (12.3% of Class A) and 5,569,053,075 Class B shares (93.6% of Class B, including 350,000,000 Class B shares issuable upon option exercise within 60 days), combining for **85.1% of pre-offering voting power**.
- **Post-IPO exact percentage is NOT determinable:** The filing states: *"Elon Musk… will hold approximately [blank] % of the voting power of our common stock (or approximately [blank] % if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full) immediately after the completion of this offering…"*

**Conclusion:** The filing as filed definitively establishes that Musk will retain majority post-IPO voting control (a qualitative, structural conclusion). The precise post-IPO voting percentage requires amendment disclosure because it depends on the undisclosed offering size and share count.

---

### Summary Table

| Item | Determinable as Filed? | Amendment Required? |
|------|----------------------|---------------------|
| (i) Post-IPO fully diluted share count | **No** — cover page and capitalization blanks; fee-table share count is a fee estimate only | **Yes** |
| (ii) Class A vs. Class B economic ownership (post-IPO) | **No** — post-offering beneficial ownership columns blank; pre-IPO split (55.30% A / 44.70% B) is calculable but post-IPO is not | **Yes** (for post-IPO figures) |
| (iii) Musk's post-IPO voting control | **Partially** — qualitative majority control is definitively stated; pre-IPO 85.1% disclosed; exact post-IPO % is blank | **Yes** (for exact post-IPO percentage only) |

The key analytical distinction: the S-1's dual-class architecture and Musk's pre-IPO holdings (93.6% of Class B, 85.1% voting power) are sufficient to conclude as a matter of disclosed fact that he will retain majority voting control post-IPO. But any precise post-IPO figure — share count, economic ownership split, or voting percentage — depends on the offering size, which the preliminary prospectus deliberately omits and which must be supplied in an amendment.

---

      "name": "Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (SpaceX) Form S-1 Registration Statement, filed May 20, 2026"
    }

The cover page, beneficial ownership tables, and certain risk-factor disclosures contain blank placeholders ("$________", "________ shares", "________%") for offering-price, share-count, and post-IPO percentage figures. ### (i) Post-IPO Fully Diluted Share Count — **REQUIRES AMENDMENT DISCLOSURE**

  - "Class A common stock outstanding immediately after this offering … ________ shares (or ________ shares if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares…)."
  - "Class B common stock outstanding immediately after this offering … ________ shares."
- The number of Class A shares offered, the over-allotment option size, and any concurrent private placements are all blank.
- The fee table registers **66,666,670 shares** with proposed maximum aggregate offering prices escalating from $5.5 trillion to $7.5 trillion — but these are bona fide estimates used solely for calculating the SEC registration fee, not the actual offering size. They do not yield a determinable post-IPO share count.

This establishes the pre-IPO base but not the post-IPO fully diluted count, which depends on the (undisclosed) offering size, greenshoe exercise, and any RSU/option settlement at closing.

- **Pre-IPO economic split is calculable from disclosed share counts:**

The filing states the post-offering percentages are "based on [blank] shares of our Class A common stock and [blank] shares of our Class B common stock outstanding… after giving effect to the sale of [blank] shares of Class A common stock in this offering."

**Conclusion:** The economic equivalence of the two classes is disclosed, and the pre-IPO economic split is calculable. However, the post-IPO Class A vs. Class B economic ownership split requires amendment disclosure because it depends on the undisclosed offering size.

### (iii) Musk's Post-IPO Voting Control — **PARTIALLY DETERMINABLE (qualitative yes; exact percentage requires amendment)**

**The qualitative fact of majority voting control IS determinable; the exact percentage is NOT.**

- **Definitive qualitative disclosure (determinable as filed):** The S-1 states unambiguously: *"Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (the Class A common stock and the Class B common stock voting together) and therefore will be able to elect all the members of our board."*
- **Structural mechanisms supporting this conclusion are disclosed:**
  - Class B carries 10 votes/share vs. Class A's 1 vote/share.
  - Class B holders, voting separately as a class, elect 51% of authorized directors.
  - Musk can only be removed from his board/leadership roles by a majority vote of outstanding Class B shares.
  - The company will be a "controlled company" under Nasdaq/Nasdaq Texas rules.
- **Pre-IPO voting power IS disclosed:** Musk holds 849,494,440 Class A shares (12.3% of Class A) and 5,569,053,075 Class B shares (93.6% of Class B, including 350,000,000 Class B shares issuable upon option exercise within 60 days), combining for **85.1% of pre-offering voting power**.
- **Post-IPO exact percentage is NOT determinable:** The filing states: *"Elon Musk… will hold approximately [blank] % of the voting power of our common stock (or approximately [blank] % if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full) immediately after the completion of this offering…"*

**Conclusion:** The filing as filed definitively establishes that Musk will retain majority post-IPO voting control (a qualitative, structural conclusion). The precise post-IPO voting percentage requires amendment disclosure because it depends on the undisclosed offering size and share count.

---

### Summary Table

| Item | Determinable as Filed? | Amendment Required? |
|------|----------------------|---------------------|
| (i) Post-IPO fully diluted share count | **No** — cover page and capitalization blanks; fee-table share count is a fee estimate only | **Yes** |
| (ii) Class A vs. Class B economic ownership (post-IPO) | **No** — post-offering beneficial ownership columns blank; pre-IPO split (55.30% A / 44.70% B) is calculable but post-IPO is not | **Yes** (for post-IPO figures) |
